info.title: Admin Control API
info.description: API to manage policies and resources. A policy is a set of rules that apply to a specific area and a particular group, allowing an organization to ensure a basic level of compliance, security, and behavior. A resource refers to any asset, entity, or item that the policy aims to govern, regulate, or protect.

tags.name: Policies
tags.description: A policy is a set of rules that apply to a specific area and a particular group, allowing an organization to ensure a basic level of compliance, security, and behavior. All policies have their own distinct set of rules and specifies the types of resources that can be associated with it. All APIs marked as deprecated will be temporarily disabled while we make updates to them.

tags.name: Resources
tags.description: A resource refers to any asset, entity, or item that the policy aims to govern, regulate, or protect.

tags.name: Authentication Policies
tags.description: Authentication policies are a set of rules that govern the authentication process for users. [Understand authentication policies](https://support.atlassian.com/security-and-access-policies/docs/understand-authentication-policies/)

get.description:
Returns comprehensive details on organizational policies, including both rules and resources.

#### Scopes
**[OAuth 2.0 scopes](/cloud/admin/scopes/) required:** `read:policies:admin`

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Your organization is identified by a Unique ID. You get your organization ID and Organization API key simultaneously.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Sets the starting point for the page of results to return.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Sets the type for the page of policies to return.

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax.

404.description: The server cannot find the requested resource.

documents.title: About
documents.body:
The admin control APIs provide a comprehensive suite of tools that allow you to manage and govern your organizational entity, including:
- Establishing policies
- Modifying policies
- Associating resources with your policies

**Permission:** Organization admin

documents.title: Authentication
documents.body:
Authentication is implemented via an API key. Use the API Key as a Bearer access token to authenticate the script and manage your organization. The Organization ID is used as a unique identifier in the path parameter for the organization. Copy the Organization ID and API key and keep them in a secure place. We won’t show them to you again.

To make requests to the API with the client/tool of your choice, [Create an API key and get the organization ID](https://support.atlassian.com/organization-administration/docs/manage-an-organization-with-the-admin-apis/).

documents.title: Rate limits
documents.body:
A rate limit defines the maximum number of API calls an application or user can perform during a specific time frame. When this threshold is surpassed, or if CPU or total time constraints are exceeded, the application or user might encounter delays. Consequently, API requests initiated by the user or application will not succeed.

It is essential to note that all API requests are subjected to rate limits.

When a client breach the rate limit thresholds, they get [HTTP 429 Too Many Requests](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6585#section-4) responses. The client has to wait for the rate limit counter to reset on the server before being able to make successful requests.

**Rate limit response headers**

The following HTTP response headers are returned indicating the rate and threshold allowed for a client for each endpoint.
- **X-RateLimit-Limit:** The number of allowed requests in the current period
- **X-RateLimit-Remaining:** The number of remaining requests in the current period
- **X-RateLimit-Reset:** Next period reset time (ISO 8601 - Date and time in UTC)

documents.title: Version and URI
documents.body:
The URIs for resources have the following structure:

```
https://api.atlassian.com/admin/<version>/policies/<resource-name>
```

documents.title: Pagination
documents.body: The admin control REST API uses pagination to conserve server resources and limit response size. If there are more results available after the current page, a link to the next page of results is included in the JSON. You can use the `cursor` parameter to set a specific starting point for the results.

documents.title: Experimental features
documents.body: Methods marked as experimental may change without an earlier notice. We are looking for your feedback for these methods.

documents.title: Vocabulary
documents.body: There are several concepts that are common ideas with a unique meaning in Atlassian Administration. Learn more about [terminology](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/admin/cloud-admin-vocabulary/) related to the cloud admin experience.

Models.ApplicationStatus.description: Status of policy application on resource

blockPageExport.description: Whether to enforce blocking page export

appAccess.description: Prevent apps from accessing your data

blockAll.description: Prevent all apps from access to your data

blockedApps.description: List apps to prevent from accessing your data

allowedApps.description: List apps to allow access to your data

block.description: Block all public link sharing from your data

anonymousAccess.description: Prevent anonymous users from accessing your data

block.description: Block anonymous access to your data

addedBy.description: The user ARI of the user who first enforced the rule on the policy

addedAt.description: Timestamp of when the rule was first enforced on the policy

lastUpdatedBy.description: The user ARI of the user who last edited the rule on the policy

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the rule was last edited on the policy

id.description: A unique identifier for this particular occurrence of the error.

status.description: The HTTP status code applicable to this error.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the error.

Models.Errors.NotFound.description: The server cannot find the requested resource.

prev.description: URL to fetch the Previous Page

next.description: URL to fetch the Next Page

page_size.description: Number of items in this page

users.description: List of email addresses of the users to be added to the policy.

accountIds.description:
Unique ID of the user's account.
Use the [Search for users in an organization API](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/admin/organization/rest/api-group-users/#api-v1-orgs-orgid-users-search-post) to get the accountIds.

page_size.description: Number of items in a page

resourceId.description: Resource ARI associated with a policy

resourceName.description: Name of resource associated with a policy

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the resource was first associated with the policy

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the resource was last associated with the policy

projectType.description: The type of project of the resource

applicationStatus.description: Status of policy application on resource

default.description: The default logo url of the resource

16x16.description: The smallest logo url of the resource

24x24.description: The small logo url of the resource

32x32.description: The medium logo url of the resource

48x48.description: The regular logo url of the resource

operation.description: The operation to perform on the resource with the policy

resourceAri.description: List of resource ARIs to be associated to or dissociated from a policy
